title: Request to NARA re: DEA support for 1992 TV miniseries "Drug Wars: The Cocaine Cartel"

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

All documents pertaining to collaboration between the Drug Enforcement Administration (DEA) and the production of the 1992 TV miniseries "Drug Wars: The Cocaine Cartel." Such documents may include but should not be limited to: script notes; production assistance agreements; photographs; and all records of communications between employees of the DEA and members of the cast, crew, and production company of "Drug Wars: The Cocaine Cartel," including memos, notes of meetings and conference calls, emails, and other forms of communication

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Brett Carollo

title: Phone and text message records between police and ICE in Massachusetts (Boston Police Department)

communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern: 

Pursuant to the Massachusetts Public Records Law, I hereby request the following records:  

1. All departmental or municipal policies regarding cell phone use and procedures for retaining cell phone and landline phone data. The records request includes, but is not limited to, policies regarding: 

*   Officer or employee use of cellphones 
*       Officer use of social media 
*       Records retention practices of phone logs and text messages 
*       Mobile device (i.e. cell phone) management 
*       Authorized software & internet usage on department-issued devices 
*       Bringing your own device 

This request covers any policies that are currently in effect or that have been in effect at any point on or after January 20, 2025. 

2. Current contract and most recent procurement records related to managing mobile phone devices within your department. This includes information about the mobile device management system (e.g. Microsoft Intune, Panasonic Connect, etc.)  that is used by the department to manage employee cell phone and computer communications, as well as screenshots of how department mobile technology is centrally managed. 

3. Call logs, if any, from January 20, 2025, to the day this request is responded to, between a department phone (including both department provided cell phones and landlines) and the following numbers: 

*   (802) 872-6020   
*   (781) 359-7500  
*   (860) 244-0522 
*   (413) 221-1303 
*   (857) 416-3742 
*   (781) 359-7451 
*   (703) 725-5982 
*   (781) 359-7553 
*   (781) 917-5143 
*   (857) 416-3705 
*   (603) 493-4520 

  Any other phone number known by the department to be a phone contact for Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) or Customs and Border Patrol (CBP). 

4. Text message records from department issued cell phones (including SMS, RCS, and iMessage) communications sent or received on department issued mobile devices from January 20, 2025, to the date this request is responded to, that contain any of the following terms: 

*       ICE 
*       CBP 
*       Immigration 
*       Immigrant 
*       Detainer 

This request includes messages stored on devices themselves, in any backup system, cloud storage, mobile device management platform, or held by any third-party vendor on behalf of the department. 

I also request that, if appropriate, fees be waived as this request is in the public interest. I expect the request to be filled in an accessible format, including for screen readers, which provide text-to-speech for persons unable to read print. Files that are not accessible to screen readers include, for example, .pdf image files as well as physical documents. In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available.  

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 10 business days, as the statute requires.  I am happy to discuss this request with your staff to help clarify any matters. 

 

Sincerely, 

Joshua Dankoff 
Director of Strategic Initaitives 
Citizens for Juvenile Justice 
joshuadankoff@cfjj.org  
(submitted via muckrock)

title: Navy support for unknown TV series "The Watch Triad"

communications.subject: Freedom of Information Act Request: Navy support for unknown TV series "The Watch Triad"
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

All documents pertaining to collaboration between the US Navy and the production of "The Watch Triad," season 1. Such documents may include but should not be limited to: script notes; production assistance agreements; photographs; and all records of communications between employees of the Navy and members of the cast, crew, and production company of "The Watch Triad," including memos, notes of meetings and conference calls, emails, and other forms of communication.

"The Watch Triad" appears on a list of Navy-supported productions for the year 2024 that was released to me through FOIA. That list is attached to this request.

I am the Director of Research Operations at a 501(c)(3) non-profit organization called The Decoding Culture Foundation as well as the editor-in-chief and a contributor to a research magazine published by said foundation titled "Cultural Engineering Studies." These documents will be used for public education and research purposes. I therefore request a waiver of all fees that may otherwise apply. Attached is a document with organization letterhead confirming my position, as well as the organization's 501(c)(3) application and approval form.

The requested documents will be made available to the general public, and this request is not being made for commercial purposes.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 20 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Brett Carollo

title: County Assessor's Tax Roll Request

communications.subject: Michigan Freedom of Information Act Request: County Assessor's Tax Roll Request
communications.communication:
Dear Open Records FOIA Coordinator,

Pursuant to Michigan's Freedom of Information Act, MCL 15.231 et seq., I am requesting a copy of the Open Records county-wide assessment roll in its existing electronic format (CSV, DBF, or equivalent tabular export). If a single county-wide file does not exist, individual municipal-level exports that together cover all parcels in the county will satisfy this request.

Requested fields include, to the extent they exist in the record: parcel number, property address, owner name and mailing address, assessed value, state equalized value, taxable value, property classification, homestead/PRE status, school district, and any recorded sale date and sale price. I understand that many counties routinely provide similar datasets to commercial data aggregators such as Zillow and other real estate platforms, and I am requesting the same or comparable data.

I ask that this request be interpreted liberally, consistent with MCL 15.231(2). To the extent additional non-exempt fields exist in the record beyond those listed above, please include them. If the records are available for multiple years or assessment cycles, please include as broad a duration as is readily available rather than limiting the response to the current year alone. Where any question arises about whether to include or exclude non-exempt information, I respectfully ask that you err on the side of inclusion.

I am not requesting GIS data, parcel geometry, or any record that must be created — only a copy of existing electronic records. Please note that MCL 15.443(4) provides that the Enhanced Access to Public Records Act does not limit inspection and copying of public records under FOIA.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you.
Alexander Munro
Ypsilanti, MI
734-272-6612
awmunro@gmail.com

communications.subject: Washington Public Records Act Request: Google Workspace Organizational Units and Microsoft Groups
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Washington Public Records Act, I hereby request the following records:

The list and structure of the Organizational Units your agency has in your Google Workspace configuration, including sub-units. The easiest way to retrieve this is probably using GAM (https://github.com/GAM-team/GAM) by using the command "gam print orgs". Alternatively, it may be listed in the Google Admin console, but from my understanding it may be more difficult to retrieve the structure from that.

The list and structure of your Microsoft 365 Groups and Microsoft 365 Security Groups. This should be in the Microsoft 365 Admin Center and can be exported to a csv.

Personal student information may be excluded. If it makes things simpler, I do not need a comprehensive list of every single individual and accounts that are in the Organizational Units or Groups. title: Flock Safety / ALPR Surveillance Records – City of Newburgh

communications.subject: New York Freedom of Information Law Request: Flock Safety / ALPR Surveillance Records – City of Newburgh
communications.communication:
Dear Records Custodian,

Under the provisions of the New York Freedom of Information Law (FOIL), N.Y. Public Officers Law §§ 84–90, I hereby request access to and copies of records held by the City of Newburgh, Orange County, relating to the planning, proposal, procurement, authorization, or attempted deployment of Flock Safety surveillance cameras or automated license plate reader (ALPR) systems.

This request is made in the public interest to better understand the use, consideration, or implementation of surveillance technologies by local government entities, including decision-making processes, vendor relationships, and potential impacts on public privacy.

The purpose of this request is to determine the origin, authorization process, procurement activity, proposed locations, and communications surrounding any surveillance camera system, including identifying which individual, agency, or vendor first initiated discussions or planning for such a program within the City of Newburgh.

Timeframe: January 1, 2023 – Present, including any earlier records referencing or relating to Flock Safety, automated license plate readers, or surveillance cameras within the City of Newburgh.

Records Requested

For the purpose of this request, the records I seek include, but are not limited to the following:

1. Contracts and Agreements

All contracts, agreements, memoranda of understanding, purchase orders, vendor agreements, procurement documents, or proposals involving the City of Newburgh and Flock Safety or any related entities concerning surveillance cameras, automated license plate readers, or related monitoring technology.

This includes draft contracts, executed agreements, amendments, pilot program agreements, trial program documentation, cancellation notices, procurement justifications, and contract termination records.

Legal Basis: Contracts involving public funds or public infrastructure are public records subject to disclosure under N.Y. Public Officers Law § 87.

2. Records Identifying Project Initiation

All records reflecting the initial introduction, proposal, solicitation, or outreach regarding Flock Safety cameras or ALPR systems, including records identifying:

• the individual or office that first proposed or recommended the system  
• the first communications between the City and Flock Safety  
• vendor outreach to city officials  
• internal discussions about introducing the technology  
• marketing or sales outreach from vendors  

3. Internal Communications

All internal communications including emails, memoranda, text messages, chat messages, letters, meeting notes, or other correspondence among:

• the Mayor  
• City Council members  
• City of Newburgh Police Department personnel  
• City administrative staff  
• City legal counsel  
• Public Works or Planning Department personnel  
• other municipal offices  

that discuss:

• Flock Safety  
• automated license plate readers  
• surveillance cameras  
• camera deployments  
• vendor procurement or negotiations  
• surveillance technology adoption  

4. Communications With External Entities

All communications between the City of Newburgh and:

• representatives of Flock Safety  
• technology vendors or consultants  
• Orange County officials  
• local, state, or federal law enforcement agencies  
• regional intelligence or real-time crime centers  

concerning surveillance cameras, ALPR systems, or the Flock Safety program.

5. Vendor Demonstrations and Sales Outreach

All records relating to vendor presentations, product demonstrations, marketing materials, or sales outreach regarding Flock Safety or related surveillance technology, including:

• presentation decks  
• vendor proposals  
• demonstration scheduling emails  
• meeting invitations or call summaries  
• webinar invitations  
• follow-up communications  

6. Proposed Camera Locations and Site Selection

All records identifying, evaluating, or discussing proposed or potential locations for surveillance cameras or automated license plate reader systems within the City of Newburgh.

This includes but is not limited to:

• maps showing proposed camera placements  
• GIS maps or infrastructure maps  
• deployment plans  
• proposed installation locations or intersections  
• roadway monitoring points  
• entry or exit routes to the city  
• communications discussing site selection  
• vendor recommendations for placement  
• field surveys or site inspections  
• infrastructure or engineering assessments related to camera placement  

7. Deployment and Planning Documents

All planning or operational records relating to the surveillance system, including:

• deployment plans  
• camera location maps  
• infrastructure assessments  
• equipment specifications  
• installation schedules  
• operational capability descriptions  
• technology architecture documents  

8. Financial and Procurement Records

All records relating to funding, budgeting, or procurement of the surveillance system, including:

• invoices  
• payment records  
• purchase authorizations  
• budget allocations  
• grant applications  
• cost analyses  
• procurement justifications  
• financial approvals  

9. Meeting Records

All agendas, minutes, recordings, transcripts, or notes from City Council meetings, committee meetings, departmental meetings, or internal meetings where surveillance camera programs or Flock Safety systems were discussed, proposed, approved, or canceled.

10. Oversight, Audit, and Compliance Records

Any audit reports, legal opinions, compliance reviews, investigations, or internal analyses relating to surveillance camera programs or procurement processes.

11. Data Access, Sharing, and Integration

All records relating to data access or sharing involving the surveillance system, including:

• agreements allowing other agencies to access camera data  
• policies governing license plate reader searches  
• records concerning integration with regional surveillance networks  
• data retention policies  
• privacy or civil liberties assessments  

Electronic Records and Metadata

For all responsive electronic records, including emails and digital documents, I request that records be produced in their native electronic format where possible, including all available metadata and email header information.

This includes, but is not limited to:

• sender and recipient information  
• CC and BCC fields  
• full email header routing data  
• timestamps for sending and receipt  
• message ID fields  
• attachment metadata  
• document creation dates  
• last modified dates  
• author fields  
• file path information where available  

Providing metadata is necessary to determine the origin, timing, and authorship of communications relating to the surveillance camera proposal.

Request for Segregable Non-Exempt Portions

Should any portion of the requested records be deemed exempt from disclosure, I request the release of any reasonably segregable non-exempt portions in accordance with N.Y. Public Officers Law § 87(2).

Preservation of Records

Please note your obligation not to destroy or alter any records potentially responsive to this request. Once a FOIL request has been received, agencies are required to preserve all responsive records during processing.

Fee Waiver Request

I request a fee waiver for the processing of this request as the information sought is in the public interest and its disclosure will contribute significantly to public understanding of government surveillance programs and decision-making processes.

If potential fees exceed $200, please inform me before proceeding.

Public Availability of Communications

Please be advised that communications relating to this request, including incoming and outgoing correspondence, may be publicly published on transparency platforms such as MuckRock.com.

Statutory Response Time

As required under N.Y. Public Officers Law § 89(3), I look forward to acknowledgment of this request within five business days.

Sincerely,

Jordan Lassiter  
Independent Investigative Journalist

communications.subject: Connecticut Freedom of Information Act Request: AER - CT Dept. of Public Health
communications.communication:
To Whom It May Concern:

Pursuant to the Connecticut Freedom of Information Act, I hereby request the following records:

All adverse event records filed with the Connecticut Department of Public Health between Jan. 1, 2023 and Jan. 1, 2025.

I request these records in their native digital formats and not in formats that degrade the accessibility of the records, such as tabular data converted to PDF or Word files. If converting the native digital format to another high-fidelity format, such as CSV or Excel, please take care not to introduce formatting inconsistencies or errors, which can occur in the conversion process (for example, encoding errors or the inclusion of internal unescaped quotation marks in CSV files). Should the records above represent certain values as numeric codes or abbreviations that are not self-explanatory, we additionally request all records necessary to interpret those codes and abbreviations.

I also request that, if possible and pursuant to Conn. Gen. Stat. § 1-212(d), fees be waived as I believe this request is in the public interest and not made for commercial gain. The requested documents will be made available to the public at MuckRock.com.

In the event that there are fees, I would be grateful if you would inform me of the total charges in advance of fulfilling my request. I would prefer the request filled electronically, by e-mail attachment if available or CD-ROM if not.

Thank you in advance for your anticipated cooperation in this matter. I look forward to receiving your response to this request within 4 business days, as the statute requires.

Sincerely,

Michael Nolan
